ALIYAH: I was returning to the terminal. That's, uh, that's the protocol. Log the truck in, do the post-trip inspection, drop the keys. Before I, you know, before I clock out.

ALIYAH: No ma'am. Hands-free Bluetooth is the only thing we're allowed to do, and I wasn't even on a call. I was just driving.

assessments.reasoning: DOC-001 states the policy period runs from 01/01/2023 to 01/01/2024. The loss date of 03/13/2023 falls squarely within that window.

assessments.reasoning: DOC-001 schedules Unit 14 explicitly under the CA 99 03 endorsement schedule and lists it with Liability coverage under Symbol 7. DOC-002 confirms the same vehicle — same VIN-consistent tag — was operated at the time of loss.

DOC-001 requires a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14 and three years of commercial driving experience; Aliyah has four years with Northwind per DOC-003. The file does not contain the MVR itself, introducing a small residual uncertainty, but all available evidence points to compliance.

assessments.claim_text: Claimant Reyes sustained bodily injury in the accident, triggering the auto BI coverage part.
assessments.reasoning: DOC-002 records Reyes complaining of neck and lower-back pain at scene and being transported to Tampa General, coded 'Possible Injury (C).' The injury is documented but medical records have not yet been received, so the nature and extent remain unverified at this stage.

assessments.claim_text: There are no apparent policy exclusions or conditions that would bar coverage for this exposure.
assessments.reasoning: The policy covers specifically described autos for BI liability with no applicable deductible. The loss was caused by a rear-end collision during on-duty commercial use of the scheduled vehicle. No excluded operations (e.g., pollution, intentional acts) are implicated. No alcohol or drug use is noted by either DOC-002 or DOC-003. The UM rejection is noted but is irrelevant to a first-party BI liability claim by a third-party claimant. The 6% residual reflects the absence of the full policy form text and any manuscript endorsements not listed on the declarations.

outcomes.claim_text: Clean coverage — BI liability coverage applies without reservation.
outcomes.reasoning: All primary coverage triggers are satisfied: in-policy-period loss, specifically described covered auto, authorized on-duty driver with valid CDL, and documented third-party bodily injury arising from a covered accident. No exclusion or condition appears to be implicated on the current record. The $1,000,000 CSL limit applies with a $0 liability deductible.

outcomes.claim_text: Reservation of Rights — coverage is likely but one or more issues require further investigation before a clean coverage confirmation.
outcomes.reasoning: Residual uncertainty arises from: (1) the MVR for Aliyah not being in the file — if the annual MVR pull required by the declarations reveals a disqualifying violation, a driver-eligibility issue could surface; (2) the full CA 00 01 policy form and all endorsements are not in the file — any manuscript exclusion or condition not listed on the declarations is unverified; (3) Aliyah's CDL class is listed as 'A2241' (Class A) in the police report, while the declarations require 'CDL Class B' for Unit 14 — a potential technical misalignment worth confirming, though holding a Class A implicitly satisfies a Class B requirement under FMCSA hierarchy.

Driver: Aliyah, Marcus J., M/47, FL CDL A2241, DOB 12/04/1975.

outcomes.reasoning: Denial is a remote possibility on the current record. It would require a finding that either (a) Unit 14 was not in fact a covered auto (contradicted by DOC-001 and DOC-002), (b) Aliyah was operating outside authorized use (contradicted by DOC-003 and DOC-004), or (c) a previously unknown exclusion or material misrepresentation surfaces. No present evidence supports any of these grounds.

would_shift_distribution: Receipt and review of the full CA 00 01 policy form and all endorsements not yet in the file — any undisclosed exclusion could shift mass toward ROR or denial.
Aliyah's current MVR — a disqualifying violation or CDL suspension could raise a driver-eligibility question and shift mass toward ROR.
Confirmation of Aliyah's CDL class (Class A vs. Class B requirement in declarations) — if Class A satisfies Class B per FMCSA, this resolves in favor of clean coverage.
Tampa General medical records for Reyes — would clarify nature and extent of injury and confirm the BI trigger.
Any evidence of unauthorized personal use or route deviation by Aliyah — would shift mass toward ROR or denial.
Any prior knowledge of brake or braking-distance defects on Unit 14 documented in maintenance records — could implicate a maintenance/negligent entrustment theory but would not negate coverage.

coverage_analysis_memo.body:
COVERAGE ANALYSIS MEMORANDUM

File No.: CLM-2023-04471
Exposure: EXP-2023-04471-REYES-BI
Policy: NWL-CA-024471 (Northwind Logistics, LLC)
Coverage Part: CP-NWL-2023-BI — Auto Bodily Injury Liability
Claimant: J. Reyes (PTY-CLM-REYES)
Loss Date: March 13, 2023
Prepared: 2025-01-31

─────────────────────────────────────────
I. COVERAGE FRAMEWORK
─────────────────────────────────────────
Policy NWL-CA-024471 is a CA 00 01 (ISO Commercial Auto) form issued to Northwind Logistics, LLC. The declarations page (DOC-001) confirms the policy period runs from "01/01/2023 12:01 A.M. to 01/01/2024 12:01 A.M. (Standard Time)." The loss date of 03/13/2023 is within the policy period.

The liability coverage is structured as a Combined Single Limit of $1,000,000 per accident with a $0 deductible, as stated in DOC-001: "Bodily Injury / Property Damage Combined Single Limit ..... $1,000,000 / Each Accident $1,000,000 / Deductible (Liability) ... $0."

Covered autos are designated under Symbol 7 (Specifically Described Autos). The full schedule is attached via endorsement CA 99 03.

─────────────────────────────────────────
II. COVERED AUTO
─────────────────────────────────────────
Unit 14, the 2019 Freightliner M2-106 box truck involved in this loss, is expressly scheduled as a covered auto in DOC-001: "Unit 14: 2019 Freightliner M2-106, VIN 1FVACWDT9KHKL4218, GVWR 24,500 lbs, Body: Box truck (24-ft), Garage: Tampa terminal ... Use: pickup/delivery — general commodities. Liability + Comp + Collision."

DOC-002 (police report) independently confirms: "V-1: 2019 Freightliner M2-106 (24-ft box truck, no trailer), tag FL-TRK-Q4218. Owner: Northwind Logistics LLC. Insurer: per policy NWL-CA-024471."

The covered auto element is satisfied.

─────────────────────────────────────────
III. AUTHORIZED USE / INSURED STATUS OF DRIVER
─────────────────────────────────────────
Driver Aliyah was the assigned operator of Unit 14 on the day of loss. DOC-004 states: "Authorization: Marcus Aliyah is the assigned driver of Unit 14 for the 2023-03-13 BR-Mon-A route. No reassignment, no swap. Vehicle use during the return leg (Adamo → Causeway terminal) is on-duty per Northwind operations manual §4.3 (truck must be returned to terminal before driver clock-out)."

At the time of impact (16:42), Aliyah had completed his final delivery stop at 15:51 (DOC-004: "12 / 15:51 / 4471 E Adamo Dr (warehouse A) / delivery / completed [final drop]") and was en route back to the Causeway terminal via Causeway Blvd — the direct and expected return route per DOC-003: "Causeway is the route back to the terminal. There's not really another way to get there from the Adamo warehouse."

No personal errand, deviation, or unauthorized use is evidenced in the file. The return leg is expressly classified as on-duty by Northwind's operations manual (DOC-004).

─────────────────────────────────────────
IV. DRIVER ELIGIBILITY
─────────────────────────────────────────
DOC-001 requires: "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience."

DOC-004 records Aliyah's license as "CDL #A2241-FL, exp. 2024-08-31" — valid on the loss date. DOC-002 records him as "FL CDL A2241." The prefix 'A' in the license number (not the CDL class designation) is noted; the police report does not specify CDL class. Because a CDL Class A license encompasses all Class B vehicle privileges under FMCSA regulations, the Class A/B distinction is unlikely to create an eligibility gap, but the actual MVR has not been received and should be confirmed.

Aliyah is 47 years old (DOB 12/04/1975 per DOC-002), well above the age 23 threshold. He has approximately four years with Northwind (DOC-003: "this is my fourth year"), satisfying the three-year experience requirement.

Annual MVR confirmation is a condition noted on the declarations. The file does not contain the MVR itself; this should be obtained to close the eligibility question.

─────────────────────────────────────────
V. NATURE OF LOSS / BI TRIGGER
─────────────────────────────────────────
DOC-002 documents a rear-end collision at 16:42 on 03/13/2023 at Causeway Blvd / Bermuda Ave, Tampa, FL 33619 — within the policy's geographic jurisdiction. Claimant Reyes "complained of neck and lower-back pain on scene; transported non-emergency by HCFR to Tampa General. Coded 'Possible Injury (C).'" (DOC-002). Medical records from Tampa General have not yet been received and remain outstanding.

Aliyah was cited for FSS 316.0895(1) — Following Too Closely (DOC-002). No alcohol, no drugs, no distraction were coded as contributing factors (DOC-002; DOC-003).

─────────────────────────────────────────
VI. APPLICABLE LIMIT AND DEDUCTIBLE
─────────────────────────────────────────
CP-NWL-2023-BI: $1,000,000 CSL per occurrence / per accident. $0 deductible. No SIR. This exposure is a single-accident, single-claimant BI claim; the per-person sub-limit equals the per-occurrence limit at $1,000,000.

─────────────────────────────────────────
VII. POTENTIAL ISSUES AND OPEN ITEMS
─────────────────────────────────────────
1. MVR for Aliyah — not in file. Required by declarations. Should be obtained to confirm no disqualifying violation.
2. Full policy form (CA 00 01) and all endorsements — not in file. Declarations list five endorsements; review should confirm no exclusion or condition applies.
3. Tampa General medical records for Reyes — outstanding. Necessary to assess injury causation and extent.
4. CDL class confirmation — Aliyah's CDL class designation (Class A vs. Class B) should be confirmed against the declarations requirement, though Class A satisfying Class B is the expected result.
5. Unit 14 maintenance records — not requested; potentially relevant to pre-existing brake/stopping-distance issues referenced by Aliyah in DOC-003: "The truck doesn't stop like a car, you know, the box truck loaded up." Note: truck was empty at time of loss per Aliyah's statement.

─────────────────────────────────────────
VIII. COVERAGE PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────
  Clean Coverage:         90%
  Reservation of Rights:   8%
  Denial:                  2%

The dominant probability reflects a straightforward record: in-policy-period loss, scheduled covered auto, authorized on-duty driver, documented third-party bodily injury, no apparent exclusions. The ROR probability reflects the open MVR, missing full policy form, and CDL class confirmation item. The denial probability is residual only.

─────────────────────────────────────────
END OF MEMO
─────────────────────────────────────────

ror_letter.body:
RESERVATION OF RIGHTS LETTER
[DRAFT — FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW AND DECISION]

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619
Attn: [Risk Manager / Authorized Representative]

Re:   Claim No.:      CLM-2023-04471
      Policy No.:     NWL-CA-024471
      Insured:        Northwind Logistics, LLC
      Claimant:       Janet M. Reyes
      Date of Loss:   March 13, 2023
      Coverage Part:  Auto Bodily Injury Liability (CP-NWL-2023-BI)

Dear [Name]:

[CARRIER NAME] ("the Company") has received notice of the above-referenced claim arising from a motor vehicle accident on March 13, 2023 on Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, Florida, involving Unit 14 (2019 Freightliner M2-106) operated by Marcus Aliyah.

The Company is conducting a coverage investigation and will defend and, if appropriate, indemnify Northwind Logistics, LLC under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 subject to the following reservations. Nothing in this letter should be construed as a waiver of any policy term, condition, or defense, whether or not specifically identified herein.

I. POLICY PROVISIONS UNDER REVIEW

The Policy is issued on form CA 00 01 (ISO Commercial Auto). The following provisions are relevant to the Company's evaluation:

1. Covered Auto Symbol 7 — The Policy covers liability arising from the use of specifically described autos. Unit 14 is scheduled on the declarations. The Company is confirming all conditions applicable to scheduled autos are met.

2. Driver Eligibility Condition — The declarations state: "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience." (DOC-001, Declarations Page — Driver Eligibility). The Company has not yet received the current Motor Vehicle Record (MVR) for driver Aliyah. Pending receipt and review of the MVR, the Company reserves its rights with respect to the driver eligibility condition.

3. Policy Form and Endorsements — The Company has not yet reviewed the complete CA 00 01 policy form and all attached endorsements in their entirety. The Company reserves its rights with respect to any term, exclusion, or condition contained therein.

II. INVESTIGATIVE STEPS IN PROGRESS

The Company is in the process of obtaining:
  (a) The current MVR for driver Marcus Aliyah;
  (b) The complete policy form, all endorsements, and the full CA 99 03 covered auto schedule;
  (c) Medical records from Tampa General Hospital for claimant Janet M. Reyes;
  (d) Confirmation of driver Aliyah's CDL class designation.

III. CONTINUED PARTICIPATION

Notwithstanding this reservation of rights, the Company will continue to investigate the claim and provide a defense where required. The Company's participation in the defense and investigation of this matter does not constitute an admission of coverage or a waiver of any right, defense, or policy provision.

You are encouraged to retain independent counsel to advise you on your interests in light of this reservation.

Please cooperate fully with the Company's investigation as required under the Policy. If you have any questions or wish to provide additional information, please contact the assigned claim specialist.

Sincerely,

[Claims Specialist Name]
[Title]
[Carrier Name]
[Address]
[Phone / Email]

[DRAFT — THIS LETTER REQUIRES ADJUSTER REVIEW AND APPROVAL BEFORE ISSUANCE]

[CARRIER NAME] ("the Company") has completed a coverage investigation of the above-referenced claim. For the reasons set forth below, the Company has determined that coverage is not available under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 for this claim.

[NOTE TO ADJUSTER: This draft is a placeholder. On the current record, no exclusion, condition violation, or coverage gap has been established. This denial letter should only be issued if investigation reveals a specific, articulable ground — for example: (1) the MVR reveals a disqualifying CDL violation or suspension predating the loss, establishing a breach of the driver eligibility condition; (2) the full policy form review surfaces a previously unidentified exclusion directly applicable to the facts; or (3) evidence emerges that the vehicle was not in authorized use. None of these grounds are presently established. Issuing this denial on the current record would not be supported by the file evidence. The grounds below are illustrative only and must be completed with specific policy language and factual findings before this letter may be sent.]

I. POLICY PROVISION(S) AT ISSUE

II. FACTUAL AND LEGAL BASIS FOR DENIAL

III. RIGHT TO APPEAL / FLORIDA LAW

This determination is made pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Policy. You may have the right to seek review of this decision. Under Florida law, you may also have the right to file a complaint with the Florida Department of Financial Services, Division of Consumer Services, at 1-877-693-5236 or www.myfloridacfo.com.

The Company reserves all rights and defenses under the Policy and applicable law, whether or not specifically identified herein.

Sincerely,
